To resolve these addresses to function names, I'll need to fetch the debug symbols for each library using their Build IDs and then use `addr2line` to map the addresses. The most straightforward path for Android .NET MAUI crashes is to use the Build ID to locate the debug symbols, download them, and then apply `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to convert those raw addresses into readable function names.

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using debuginfod protocol
# Build IDs from the tombstone:
# libmonosgen-2.0.so: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
# libSystem.Native.so: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213
# libSystem.Globalization.Native.so: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e

SYMSERVER="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so ==="
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg \
  "${SYMSERVER}/buildid/1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/debuginfo" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "curl failed"

echo "=== Trying libSystem.Native.so ==="
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/libSystem.Native.so.dbg \
  "${SYMSERVER}/buildid/7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213/debuginfo" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "curl failed"

echo "=== Trying libSystem.Globalization.Native.so ==="
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so.dbg \
  "${SYMSERVER}/buildid/2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e/debuginfo" \
